mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an impending economic contraction, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates a broad-based demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, rising industrial production reflects supply-side capacity expansion that outpaces demand, easing inflationary pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction when UNRATE falls below 4.0%, or forward returns in high-INDPRO periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (95% CI includes zero).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate earnings potential, which directly lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.15, or the signal's sign accuracy drops below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to suppress equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns average positive values when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction that triggers wage-price spirals, prompting rate hike expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains below 2.5%, or the combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, surging consumer credit outstanding reflects pent-up demand normalization that rapidly boosts corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 1.5% month-over-month, or the forward return differential between high and low credit growth deciles is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
